page.title: chronic pancreatitis — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for chronic pancreatitis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for chronic pancreatitis: 134 alterations and 215 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Electroacupuncture at Lieque, Chize, and Zusanli can lessen the lung injury induced by severe acute pancreatitis.
Parameters of lipid peroxidation and antioxidative protection in patients with chronic pancreatitis treated by low-intensity laser therapy.
These results reveal an immunosuppressive effect of cannabinoid preparations.
Inonotus obliquus polysaccharide has strong activity for ameliorating chronic pancreatitis and a beneficial effect on gut microbiota.
Olive leaf extract protect against L-arginine induced chronic pancreatitis.
Psidium guajava flavonoids prevent NLRP3 inflammasome activation and alleviate the pancreatic fibrosis.
Pterostilbene can alleviate severe acute pancreatitis-induced tissue damage.
Puerarin could be a potential therapeutic candidate in the treatment of chronic pancreatitis.
These in vivo and in vitro studies provide novel insights regarding apigenin's mechanisms of action in reducing the severity of recurrent acute pancreatitis.
Pancreatic enzyme supplementation after surgery for chronic pancreatitis has therapeutic benefits. - Article 1

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive exocrine and endocrine insufficiency; spontaneous improvement possible in alcoholic CP with sustained abstinence (30-40% pain improvement); structural damage (fibrosis, calcifications) is irreversible
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Abstinence from alcohol in alcoholic CP: 30-40% pain improvement; endoscopic drainage of ductal obstruction: pain relief in 65-70% at 5 years; total pancreatectomy with islet autotransplantation (TPIAT): 85-90% narcotic independence at 3 years in refractory cases; antioxidant therapy: modest evidence
remission.gap_size: Large — endoscopic therapy and surgery are underused in eligible patients; most are managed with analgesics indefinitely
remission.barrier_detail: Pain management in CP often escalates to opioids without adequate trial of endoscopic decompression or TPIAT in eligible patients; TPIAT (total pancreatectomy + islet autotransplantation) for refractory pain is highly effective but available at only ~20 centres in the US; delayed referral is the dominant gap
remission.last_soc_change: 2018 — AGA/ACG chronic pancreatitis guidelines updated; endoscopic therapy (ESWL + endoscopy) vs surgery (ESCAPE trial 2020) — surgery superior at 5 years for painful CP with duct dilatation; no major pharmacotherapy change in decades
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term opioid analgesia for CP pain without endoscopic assessment (escalates dependence without addressing ductal hypertension cause in duct-obstructed patients); oral enzyme supplementation at sub-therapeutic doses (needs 50,000+ units lipase per meal); antioxidant cocktails without individualised nutritional assessment
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT) optimisation for exocrine insufficiency; smoking cessation (accelerates CP progression; underemphasised); medium-chain triglyceride diet for fat malabsorption; CGRP antagonists for visceral pain component; coeliac plexus blockade (short-term pain relief bridge)
remission.frontier_signal: TPIAT expansion (more centres; cell banking approaches); extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy (ESWL) optimisation for calcific CP; islet cell transplantation from donor pancreas for post-pancreatitis diabetes; anti-fibrotic agents (pirfenidone — Phase 2 for pancreatic fibrosis); stellate cell-targeted therapy
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
